job_title: Senior Software Engineer in Test - Mobile
job_nlp_title: Senior Software Engineer in Test - Mobile
taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Quality Assurance Engineer
core_skills: Mobile app automation, Android testing, iOS testing, Appium, Espresso
skill_match_scores: Mobile app automation:0.0, Android testing:0.0, IOS testing:0.0, Appium:0.0, Espresso:0.0
must_have_quals: 3+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor's degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience, Proven experience in mobile app automation for Android and iOS platforms, Proficiency in testing frameworks like Appium, Espresso, and XCTest, Strong programming skills in Python, NodeJS, Kotlin, or Swift, Familiarity with CI/CD tools such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or similar, Excellent problem-solving and communication skills
core_responsibilities: Delivers complete solutions spanning all phases of the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) (design, implementation, testing, delivery and operations), based on definitions from more senior roles, Advises immediate management on project-level issues, Guides junior engineers, Operates with little day-to-day supervision, making technical decisions based on knowledge of internal conventions and industry best practices, Applies knowledge of technical best practices in making decisions, Develop and maintain automated test scripts for Android and iOS applications, Build and optimize mobile testing frameworks and CI/CD pipelines, Perform functional, regression, and performance testing using automation tools, Identify, analyze, and report bugs, ensuring comprehensive test coverage, Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define quality standards and best practices
job_summary: PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for over 25 years, empowering consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets. The Senior Software Engineer in Test - Mobile will deliver complete solutions across the Software Development Lifecycle, guiding junior engineers and making technical decisions with minimal supervision.
